transcript.title: Opening
transcript.text: Good morning Rushil Bhatia. Here is your daily news for Saturday, July 18. In today's briefing: top headlines, bhopal and madhya pradesh, business and markets, money, tax and personal finance, science and technology, development and policy, across india, around the world. Let's begin.

transcript.title: Top headlines
transcript.text: First, the stories leading the news this morning. 'Rohit Sharma will continue to represent India; Lord's ODI won't be his last': BCCI. BCCI secretary Devajit Saikia has dismissed speculation about Rohit Sharma's ODI retirement. He confirmed that Rohit remains a regular member of India's ODI team. Rohit will continue to represent the country as long as he is in the scheme of things. The selectors are assessing all players for the 2027 World Cup preparations. EC can delete doubtful voters, cannot decide citizenship: Supreme Court. Seeks bengal's response on ration denial to deleted voters DMK open to talks on delimitation bill 'if Tamil Nadu interests not hurt'. Seeks 'Ironclad guarantee' on states' seats Scientists fit 75 pronghorns, fastest land animal in Americas, with GPS collars: What they found. GPS collars track pronghorn movement near a New Mexico solar project. Fences around the solar farm present a significant barrier to these animals. Pronghorn typically crawl under fences rather than jumping over them. Researchers suggest simple fence modifications could aid wildlife movement. Delimitation debate: How opposition faultlines may help govt in Monsoon session. There are strong speculations that the government is planning to reintroduce the Delimitation Bill during upcoming Monsoon Session. The Bill could not pass the numbers test during the Budget session. Opposition parties, which were united in their opposition to the Bill in April, appear to be divided now over the idea of uniform seat increase. Some parties may support the bill if their concerns are addressed. Winners will make the hard choices: Microsoft's security chief to employees after layoffs. Microsoft's new security chief Hayete Gallot has replaced senior executives, merged engineering teams, and cut hundreds of jobs to push the company toward AI-powered cybersecurity, The Information reports. In an internal memo, Gallot told staff the industry is being reimagined and rewards firms that make hard choices early. Microsoft is chasing AI security spending flowing to rivals OpenAI and Anthropic. Who is Harper Seven Beckham? Meet David and Victoria Beckham's only daughter. Harper Seven Beckham is now one of the most celebrity offspring in the world. She is the only daughter of soccer icon David Beckham and fashion designer Victoria Beckham and has lived her life in the public eye while keeping a relatively private existence. From her famous name to her fashion appearances, here's everything you need to know about Harper Seven Beckham Bullet train on track, Japan ex-min's comments 'factually incorrect': Government. NEW DELHI: Government defended its high-speed rail cooperation with Japan, dismissing the statement of a former Japanese minister blaming India for the lack of progress in the ongoing work on the Ahmedabad-Mumbai 'bullet train' project. How 58 beavers revived a valley buried by Mount St. Helens eruption. More than 40 years after the Mount St. Helens eruption buried a Washington river valley under volcanic sediment, 58 relocated beavers have helped restore the damaged landscape. Their dams and wetlands brought back native plants, fish and wildlife. However, conservationists fear a planned government project to raise a sediment retention structure could undo years of natural restoration. US' draft proposal narrows flexibility in seeking bridge finance for EB-5 projects. The US Department of Homeland Security (DHS) has proposed regulations that would tighten the treatment of 'bridge financing' under the EB-5 Programme (commonly known as the investment linked green card programme). Mom spends $17K turning basement into hockey rink for her sons. Amanda Vernaci created a hockey rink in her basement over three months. This ambitious DIY project cost approximately seventeen thousand dollars for her family. She began her DIY journey in a rental home, sharing budget-friendly projects. Her skills developed further while pregnant, teaching herself to use tools.

transcript.title: Bhopal and Madhya Pradesh
transcript.text: Now, news closer to home in Bhopal and across Madhya Pradesh. Farmers Keep Away From Selling Moong At Centres, Only 7K Tonnes Procured. Madhya Pradesh has procured only 7,000 tonnes of moong since July 1 against the Centre's target of 4.55 lakh tonnes. Pension Relief: MP, Chhattisgarh End Mutual Consent Rule For DR Hikes. Madhya Pradesh and Chhattisgarh have ended the requirement for mutual consent before revising Dearness Relief (DR) for pensioners. Umang Singhar Alleges Compensation Irregularities In Ken-Betwa Project, Seeks Probe. Leader of Opposition Umang Singhar alleged irregularities in compensation distribution under the Ken-Betwa Project, claiming payments meant for displaced families went to ineligible beneficiaries. MP High Court Hears Plea Against 11-Year Social Boycott Over Same-Gotra Marriage. A couple approached the Madhya Pradesh High Court alleging a community organisation imposed an 11-year social boycott and monetary penalties over their same-gotra marriage. Madhya Pradesh launches first international flight with direct Indore-Abu Dhabi service. MP finalises 500-post metropolitan authority set-up for Bhopal, Ujjain-Indore regions. A meeting to help small farmers in Madhya Pradesh. Theft gang back in Bhopal, motorists on target. Odisha man found dead, suicide note recovered. Bhopal got 9 percent surplus rain, 25 dists stare at growing deficit. Datia bypoll: EC allots symbols to candidates. Datia Bye Elections: BJP opens poll office, media centre in Datia. Two cousins drown while bathing in Parvati River in Nazirabad. Aishbagh double murder: Rain muffled gunshots, property papers revealed motive. Doctors on holiday save crash victim with CPR on MP highway. Ken-Betwa project-hit families intensify stir; activist's fast enters 12th day. Woman faints after finding dead lizard in amla preserve, hospitalised in MP. Dhar Bhojshala Row: Muslims Reject Alternative Namaz Site, To Move Supreme Court. The district administration offered Muslims an alternative site, about 2 km from Bhojshala, for Friday namaz following Supreme Court directions.

transcript.title: Business and markets
transcript.text: Moving to business, the economy and the markets. Beaten down IT majors catch fancy of prop traders. Selling cash shares borrowed from HNI clients , buying futures on IT stocks yields high single-digit gains for traders Netflix shares tumble 13 percent after weak sales forecast, hit 22-month low. Netflix shares fell 12.6 percent to a 22-month low after a muted revenue outlook, despite Q2 results meeting expectations. The company's revenue growth is moderating, and it narrowed its 2026 guidance amid pressures from original content performance and declining stock value since mid-2025. Sebi clears automatic SWP, STP mandates for demat mutual fund holdings. Until now, automatic instructions were available only for mutual fund units held in statement of account (SOA) form, where the fund house maintains the investment records Pulse of the Street: Earnings sentiment rescues volatile week; real test ahead. The 30-share Sensex rose 0.75 percent for the week, while the broader Nifty 50 gained 0.53 percent , with most of the gains coming on Friday. The Nifty jumped 1.1 percent to close at 24,334.30, while the Sensex advanced 1.25 percent to settle at 78,151.44, its highest closing level since 12 June 2026. Apple overtakes Nvidia as world's most valuable company amid chip selloff. Nvidia lost its title as the world's most valuable company to Apple after a selloff in semiconductor stocks. Nvidia shares fell 3.7 percent , while Apple's rose 0.4 percent , raising its market cap to $4.9 trillion amidst concerns over emerging AI competition from Chinese startups. Wall Street falls on deepening selloff in chip stocks, Netflix slumps10.6 percent , Nvidia tumbles 4 percent. As of 12:53 p.m. Eastern Time, the S and P 500 fell 0.5 percent , the Dow Jones Industrial Average was down 0.3 percent , and the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.7 percent . Reliance Q1 Results: Profit up 16 percent sans one-time income versus same quarter last year , 5 key highlights. Reliance Q1 results: Consolidated profit declined 22.40 percent YoY to rupees 20,946 crore due to a one-time gain in Q1FY26. Sequentially, RIL's Q1FY27 profit rose by 23.42 percent . RIL's revenue from operations for the June quarter of FY27 rose by 25.41 percent YoY and 4.43 percent QoQ to rupees 3,11,850 crore. Reliance Industries promoters raise stake during Q1FY26. Check details. Promoters of Reliance Industries increased their stake to 50.5 percent by June, the highest in over three years. Mutual funds also raised their holding to 10.11 percent . Analysts anticipate strong financial results driven by oil and telecom sectors despite retail challenges. IDBI Bank ups FCNR ante with 16 percent on offer. The scheme allows customers to borrow up to 12 times their deposit amount and offers indicative effective annual returns of up to 16.2 percent , depending on the investment size and tenure, according to offer documents accessed by ET. Caliber Mining and Logistics IPO: Issue received a solid response on Day 01, booked 1.3 times. Caliber Mining and Logistics' IPO showed strong initial demand, with bids for 96.54 lakh shares against 74.29 lakh shares offered. The IPO, set at rupees 402-424, will close on July 21. Grey market premium indicates a potential listing price of rupees 529, 38 percent above the issue price. Vande Bharat sleeper trains' rollout delayed. The rollout of 120 Vande Bharat sleeper trains by Indo-Russian joint venture Kinet Railway Solutions has been delayed as final train designs are yet to be approved. Oberoi Realty Q1 profit jumps 29 percent to rupees 544 crore, revenue rises 32 percent ; declares rupees 2 interim dividend. Oberoi Realty reported Q1 FY27 net profit of rupees 544 crore, up 29 percent YoY. Revenue rose 31.7 percent to rupees 1,301 crore, with EBITDA increasing 41 percent to rupees 734 crore. The company also declared an interim dividend of rupees 2 per share as it continues to see strong demand for luxury homes. RIL shifts crude mix as Iran war disrupts oil flows. Reliance Industries increased crude oil sourcing from Russia and Latin America. PB Fintech share price rises 3 percent after HDFC Mutual Fund stake raise to 5.02 percent. PB Fintech shares rose 3 percent intraday on July 17 after HDFC Mutual Fund increased its stake to 5.02 percent via an open market purchase. The fund acquired 129,068 shares, raising its holding slightly above the 5 percent threshold. Shares were bought through various mutual fund schemes. Gujarat tops NITI investment index. Gujarat, Maharashtra, and Tamil Nadu lead NITI Aayog's investment friendliness index. JSW Steel Q1 result: Net Profit doubles to rupees 4,696 crore; revenue jumps 10 percent. JSW Steel reported a Q1 FY27 net profit of rupees 4,696 crore, more than double last year's rupees 2,209 crore, driven by a 9.8 percent revenue increase to rupees 47,364 crore. The company began construction on a new 2-million-tonne steel plant in Andhra Pradesh as part of its expansion. Banks flag rupees 20 lakh loan guarantee hurdle. Banks face challenges extending collateral-free loans up to twenty lakh rupees. Tata Power to declare Q1 results 2026 on this date. Check details. Tata Power's Board of Directors will convene on July 27 to review Q1 FY27 financial results. The trading window has been closed since June 24. 'Following': India on US bill seeking 100 percent tariffs. India on Friday said it is closely monitoring a proposed US bill that seeks to impose 100 percent tariffs on countries, including India, buying Russian crude oil. This beaten-down fintech stock jumps 39 percent this week, on track for biggest weekly gain in nearly 5 years. Fino Payments Bank's shares surged 14 percent to rupees 180.75 on July 17, marking a potential weekly gain of 39 percent . Despite recovery, the stock remains down 34 percent this year, following a strong June update and increased customer engagement, though transaction throughput declined by 35 percent . India's private space industry shoots for the stars. Private companies are reshaping India's space ambitions, building rockets and satellites as the country pushes to capture a bigger share of the global space economy. BHEL's profit comeback is real. Can its order boom sustain the rally?. BHEL's first Q1 profit in seven years, stronger execution and a rupees 2.6 trillion order book have fuelled a sharp turnaround. But rising competition and a 45x valuation could test the rally. Apple unseats Nvidia as world's top-valued company. Apple was last valued at $4.88 trillion as its shares held steady, while Nvidia was roughly at $4.86 ​trillion, following a 3.5 percent decline. Manipal Hospitals is said to cut IPO valuation from $10-12 billion estimated earlier. Still, Manipal Hospitals' proposed listing is on track to be India's biggest of 2026 so far, surpassing SBI Funds' $1 billion IPO this week. Irreplaceable loss: Tribute to Garfield Sobers. Cricket legend Sir Garfield Sobers passed away in Barbados on Friday. Expert view: Indian stock market to deliver healthy double-digit returns over medium term, says Bajaj Life's CIO. Bajaj Life's CIO Srinivas Rao Ravuri forecasts promising double-digit returns for Indian equities over the medium term, emphasising improved earnings visibility and attractive valuations. Amid geopolitical fluctuations, he advocates for a balanced investment approach across market capitalisations. IPO-bound Jio dials up 9 percent profit growth in Q1. Reliance Jio Q1 Results: Jio Platforms reported a 9 percent YoY rise in Q1 FY27 net profit to rupees 7,764 crore, supported by higher ARPU, subscriber additions and digital services growth.

transcript.title: Money, tax and personal finance
transcript.text: Now to your money — taxes, savings and personal finance. Is RBI asking people to pay rupees 7,500 in tax to get rupees 1 lakh payment? Here's the truth. A fake Reserve Bank of India letter is misleading people about pending payments. It falsely claims a rupees 1 lakh payment is on hold due to transaction failure. Recipients are asked to pay rupees 7,500 as a refundable tax to release funds. The Press Information Bureau's Fact Check unit has flagged this letter as fake. Income-tax returns: Top 10 things first time taxpayers must keep in mind while filing ITR. Income-tax returns: Attention first time taxpayers! From required documents, to which forms to fill, and the deadline date , here are the top 10 important things to keep in mind while filing your ITR. Automatic EPF transfer on switching job: Who can access this facility and who will be left out?. The new EPF reforms introduce automatic fund transfers when changing jobs. This facility requires an Aadhaar-linked and KYC-compliant Universal Account Number for members. Employees in private and exempted EPF organizations will not receive this automatic transfer. Members can check their previous work history on the EPFO portal for verification. EPF Withdrawal Rules: Pulling out your PF money? Here's when it's taxable and how to keep it tax-free. Understanding these rules is essential, particularly if you are switching jobs, dealing with a financial emergency or planning to access your retirement savings before retirement. Wealth wisdom of the day: 'Understand not only the magic of compounding long-term returns, but the...': Crucial money lesson for investors from the 'Father of Indexing'. Universally known as the 'Father of Indexing', Bogle had introduced the first indexed fund for individual investors in 1976. So, when it comes to compounding, it won't be wrong to assume that Bogle knew what he was talking about when he said that investors also need to know a crucial detail beyond the magic of compounding. EPFO: Here's how first-time users can activate UAN using Umang app and Aadhaar face authetication , Stepwise guide. The EPFO has discontinued UAN activation and new generation through the portal and shifted both services to the Umang app with Aadhaar-based Face Authentication (FAT) requirement. Here's how first-time users can complete activation... ICICI Lombard denied health insurance claim, stating the policy was cancelled; policyholder wins the case in consumer court, here's why. An insurer's appeal was dismissed by the Delhi State Consumer Commission. ICICI Lombard failed to submit its written statement within the legal timeframe. This procedural lapse meant the insurer lost its defense opportunity. The policyholder's claim was then upheld based on available evidence. 8th Pay Commission ToR outlines salary, DA, pension and allowance changes: Check full list. 8th Pay Commission: The official terms of reference (ToR) outline what central government employees and pensioners can expect from the 8th CPC's recommendations. From DA hike to revised salary structure, pension, allowances and other benefits , Check full list of likely announcements here. Facing NPS remittance delays? Finance Ministry steps in with stern warning, errant officials risk penalties. The DoE reiterated that if an employee's monthly NPS contribution is credited past the established deadline, interest must be paid to the subscriber for the duration of the delay. This compensatory interest will match the prevailing PPF interest rate, which currently stands at 7.1 percent Wife seeks to stop Husband's pension over unpaid maintenance; Madras High Court rejects plea for this reason. Despite court order husband did not pay maintenance to wife; she files case to stop his pension on this ground but loses case in Madras HC for this reason. Keep reading to know why the wife lost the case in Madras High Court. Probate of Will in India explained: Rules, court procedure and how it helps avoid inheritance disputes. Probate of a Will in India confirms its validity and gives legal authority to the executor. Check and understand the process, documents required, court procedure, recent changes, and when probate can help prevent inheritance disputes. Up to 8.50 percent interest on senior citizen FD: This bank increases interest rate on 501-day special FD; check details. Unity Small Finance Bank has revised its fixed deposit interest rates. Senior citizens can now earn up to 8.50 percent on a 501-day fixed deposit. Regular customers will receive 8.00 percent interest on the same tenure. The bank also offers 7 percent interest on savings accounts for larger deposits. DA hike: If not July, when could the next Dearness Allowance increase come this year?. Dearness Allowance: Even if no DA hike is announced this month in July 2026, central government employees and pensioners could still see a second increase sometime in the second half of the year during the festive season. Can a rupees 2,000 monthly SIP really make you a crorepati? Let's do the maths. Investing just rupees 2,000 every month, an amount many people spend on leisure activities or eating out, can potentially grow into a corpus of more than rupees 1 crore. Student wins rupees 5000 compensation and a refund with 9 percent interest after hostel served sub-standard food and neglected room maintenance; Kangra district consumer commission order. When Mr Rawat from Doonga Bazaar, Kangra, Himachal Pradesh came to Noida, Uttar Pradesh to study B.Tech at IILM University, Greater Noida, for the first semester on August 17, 2023, he booked a hostel in Knowledge Park-III. Within one week of his stay, Rawat faced severe deficiencies. The food provided was sub-standard, maintenance was neglected and the promised Wi-Fi facility/password was never shared. No Flexi Cap Fund gave 10 percent returns over the last 1 year: Should investors worry? Here's what experts say. The best-performing flexi cap fund returned 9.91 percent over the past year, while only five schemes crossed the 8 percent mark. Experts say the muted performance reflects market conditions, not poor fund management. EPF Scheme 2026: New rules for exempted PF trusts, what employees must know. The Employees' Provident Funds Scheme, 2026 introduces an interest rate ceiling, mandatory digital compliance and stricter governance norms for exempted PF trusts run by eligible employers. Here's what the new rules mean for employees. The next frontier in multi-asset investing is adaptability. Diversification on paper does not always translate into diversification in practice. Inside AlphaGrep's multi-asset allocation fund and its novel proposition, where risks are adjusted in response to shifting market signals. Hospitalized? 10 essential steps for hassle-free health insurance claim settlement. Health insurance claims can become stressful during emergencies. These 10 practical steps, from hospital admission to discharge, ensure faster cashless claim settlement, avoid delays, manage documents properly and reduce financial pressure, especially during emergencies. Is your employer delaying deposit into your NPS? Know how it can impact you. Government departments must remit employee NPS contributions promptly to PFRDA. Delays in crediting NPS contributions will incur interest charges for employees. This interest will match the Public Provident Fund rate applicable at that time. Officials causing administrative delays face liability for pecuniary losses to the government. From Form 26AS to contract notes: 6 tax documents every investor should preserve. All investors should preserve key tax documents such as Form 26AS, AIS, TIS, contract notes and mutual fund statements. These records ensure accurate ITR filing and help verify income. They are necessary to claim tax benefits, calculate capital gains and handle tax notices. CBDT Notifies CII for FY2026-27: Who Can Still Claim Indexation on Property Sale?. Budget 2024 removed indexation for most assets, but eligible property sellers can still choose between the old and new capital gains tax regimes. Here's how to know which option could lower your tax bill. EPFO may extend PF benefits to self-employed, gig workers under universal scheme. EPFO is reportedly working on a voluntary provident fund scheme for self-employed individuals, gig workers and unorganised sector workers. The proposal, still under discussion, could expand retirement savings coverage beyond the organised workforce. Phone lost? Here's your step-by-step guide to protecting your money and personal data. The loss or theft of your smartphone can expose your bank accounts, UPI applications, and personal data to fraud. Check out the five essential steps to secure your SIM, banking applications, email and digital identity immediately after your phone is lost or stolen, helping minimise financial risk. Old Pension Scheme: These government employees can now opt for OPS, will more get the same benefit?. The OPS option is now available to eligible compassionate ground appointees who applied for appointment before January 1, 2004, but joined government service on or after January 1, 2004 and were consequently covered under the National Pension System (NPS). PF after retirement: When does your EPF balance stop earning interest? EPFO rules explained. Retirement does not automatically stop interest on your EPF balance. Here's how long your provident fund continues to earn interest under the EPF Scheme, 2026, based on your retirement age. Gold, silver prices today, 17 July: Check retail rates of 24K, 22K gold, 999 silver in Delhi, Mumbai and Kolkata. Gold and silver prices today, 17 July: Check the latest 24K, 22K gold and 999 silver retail rates across Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata and other major Indian cities, along with MCX market updates.

transcript.title: Science and technology
transcript.text: Next, science and technology. Still leaving your home unwatched? These 5 indoor security cameras help you keep an eye on everything. All of these security cameras offer 360-degree of monitoring. In addition to this, these cameras also offer support for night vision technology. Taylor Farms pulls iceberg lettuce from the US market after cyclosporiasis outbreak. Food producer Taylor Farms released a statement on the Cyclospora outbreak Friday, confirming that it's "voluntarily removing all iceberg lettuce sourced from central Mexico from the US market." Reuters reports that, according to a source, Taylor Farms told customers like Yum Brands owner Taco Bell and the food distributor Sysco on Thursday to pull shredded Applications close in 48 hours , here's everything Australian founders need to know about Stripe x Startup Battlefield. The window is almost shut. On August 19, eight startups will take the stage at Stripe Tour Sydney in front of investors, global press, and the Australian tech community. One startup walks away with automatic entry into TechCrunch Disrupt in San Francisco , no application, no further competition, a guaranteed spot on the world's most Scientists built a camera that can track invisible particles in 3D. A new particle detector called PLATON could replace millions of tiny detector components with a single block of light-producing material. Using a light-field camera, highly sensitive photon sensors, and AI, it reconstructs particle paths in fast, detailed 3D. Simulations suggest it could match or surpass today's best detectors while being far easier to scale. The technology may also lead to sharper PET medical scans. Semicon 2.0: Government to match VC funding in chip design, pick equity. Under the scheme unveiled on Wednesday, the government will match private investments in eligible startups in return for equity to reduce funding gaps in one of the country's most capital-intensive technology sectors. Shark's versatile ChillPill cooling system is back to its best price. Portable fans are one of the easiest ways to stay cool during the summer, and you don't have to spend much to find a decent one. If you're looking for something more versatile, though, Shark's ChillPill Personal Fan and Cooling System combines a rechargeable fan with a dry-touch evaporative mister in a design that can Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent , here's how it actually performs. From AI workflows to battery life and security, here's what it's really like to live with Vertu's luxury foldable every day. Scientists tested 39 sweeteners and found unexpected gut effects. A large laboratory study found that many commonly used sweeteners can directly change the growth of gut bacteria. Researchers identified more than 100 cases in which sweeteners behaved differently when combined with medications, caffeine, or flavorings. The combination of isosteviol and the antidepressant duloxetine was especially disruptive, reducing beneficial bacteria and overall microbial diversity. TikTok is testing an AI likeness detection tool. TikTok is starting to test an opt-in tool that scans for AI likenesses and lets creators report them to the company, as spotted by social media consultant Matt Navarra. The tool is initially being tested with "some" US creators, TikTok US spokesperson Zachary Kizer tells The Verge. YouTube has been working on a similar tool Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI's favorite second act. Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding. New "living plastic" self-destructs in just 6 days without leaving microplastics. Researchers have created self-destructing living plastic that uses engineered bacteria to completely break itself down when activated. The material degrades in just six days without creating microplastics, offering a potential new solution for single-use plastic waste. Pebble founder Eric Migicovsky says his 30-day warranty is all about trust. Pebble founder Eric Migicovsky says buyers of its new e-paper smartwatches should know what they're signing up for and trust Pebble to make things right if they run into issues, despite the short warranty. "I think the most important thing is trust," Migicovsky told me in an interview this week. "Do people trust the product The Zoom hack that says, 'Don't record me'. If every meeting, watercooler conversation, and date gets transcribed and summarized, who's actually reading any of it? This sugar-coated therapy boosted survival against deadly brain cancer by 50 percent in mice. A new experimental treatment may have found a way to outsmart glioblastoma's toughest defense: the blood-brain barrier. Researchers used sugar-coated nanoparticles to ferry genetic instructions that restore a key tumor-suppressing protein directly into brain cancer cells. In mouse studies, the therapy increased median survival by 50 percent while shrinking tumors without noticeable damage to other organs. Apple Music is getting a price hike. Apple Music is more expensive now. In the US, an individual plan now costs $11.99 per month, a $1 bump up from the previous $10.99 price. A family plan now costs $19.99 per month, up from $16.99, and a student plan costs $6.99 per month, up from $5.99. Apple, in a statement to Music Business Agility Robotics plants its flag in Tesla's backyard. Agility is opening a new training center for its Digit robots in Fremont, California. Apple's plot to crush OpenAI. Apple is suing OpenAI. The complaint is readable and intense, as these things often are, though many experts seem to think many of the allegations are just the ways things are done. So what does Apple really want here, and why is it picking such a public fight with OpenAI? On this episode of The AI-driven memory crunch jolts India's smartphone market. India's smartphone slowdown highlights how the AI boom is reshaping consumer electronics, from pricing and demand to corporate strategy. Asus' top-end 4K QD-OLED gaming monitor is $400 off. The Asus ROG Swift 32-inch 4K QD-OLED gaming monitor has almost everything I want in a high-end gaming monitor, most notably a QD-OLED panel for inky black levels and vivid colors that go beyond what's possible on an LCD screen. Amazon, Best Buy, and B and H Photo have this model on sale for $899.99 (usually $1,299.99). Apple and Google ordered to purge 'nudify' apps from App Stores. In letters sent to Apple and Google, San Francisco City Attorney David Chiu said that both companies have long been aware that they are hosting apps in violation of state law. Earth has a natural thermostat and scientists finally know how it works. Scientists have identified a hidden feedback loop that may explain how Earth has regulated its climate for tens of millions of years. As sea levels rose and fell, they changed how much phosphate reached the open ocean, affecting marine life and the amount of carbon buried beneath the seafloor. That burial removed carbon dioxide from the atmosphere, helping cool the planet. Samsung's redesigned Z Fold 8 with a wide display just leaked. Leaker Evan Blass shared images of Samsung's redesigned Galaxy Z Fold 8 just days before the July 22nd launch event where Samsung is expected to officially announce the phone. The leaked images show a shorter, wider foldable matching earlier leaks of the Z Fold 8. They also reveal some of its specs, including a Qualcomm Nuclear startup Valar Atomics in talks to raise new funding at $6B valuation. The potential deal highlights a growing trend of complex, multi-stage funding rounds that mask true entry prices. Is America ready for this quirky Jeep-looking EV that can park itself?. Are we living through a small car renaissance? There's the Slate Truck, Amble's dune buggy, and the Fiat Topolino, as well as a whole galaxy of kei cars and trucks from Japan that have their own built-in fan base. While microcar sales in the US are still as tiny as the vehicles themselves, there are I replaced my space heater and ceiling fan with one Dyson appliance. Designed for year-round comfort, the Dyson Hot+Cool HF1 combines quiet operation and simple controls with Dyson's signature bladeless design. Florida man arrested for allegedly stealing over $200,000 in crypto using Steam game malware. Federal authorities have arrested a Florida man suspected of stealing at least $220,000 in crypto through malware-infected Steam games, as reported earlier by local news outlet Local10. In the complaint, officials accuse 21-year-old Zyaire Wilkins and co-conspirators of launching eight malware-embedded games from around May 2024 to February 2026, allowing them to infect about 8,000

transcript.title: Development and policy
transcript.text: Now to development, infrastructure and government policy. Japan ex-minister claims 'recklessness' on bullet train project, India rejects. India reacts to Japanese ex-minister's rebuke over bullet train delay, says it's 'at variance with facts'. India to blame for Bullet Train project delay? Government junks Japan ex-minister's claim. 'Sheer recklessness': Ex-Japan minister slams India for delays in bullet train project, MEA hits back India News. #MCExclusive 'India's Real Deep State Is an Unaccountable Network of Senior Bureaucrats': Surjit Bhalla 🗣️ Economist Surjit Bhalla argued that India's "real deep state" is an unaccountable, non-transparent network of senior bureaucrats that shapes econo. India attracts students from 173 countries as overseas enrolment rises 19 percent. India's first and deepest geothermal wells commissioned in Ladakh's Puga Valley. All aboard? Not really: Japan former minister rebukes India for delays in Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train project. Protocol in Every Pocket: AI at India's Healthcare Frontline. PM Modi flags off India's first Hydrogen train in Jind.

transcript.title: Across India
transcript.text: Turning to the big national stories. They reopened their books and rewrote their NEET story. Medical aspirants demonstrated remarkable resilience after the NEET EC can delete doubtful voters, cannot decide citizenship: SC. Seeks bengal's response on ration denial to deleted voters Congress warns Punjab dissidents against indiscipline, armtwisting, gives no assurances. Congress leaders were sternly told to maintain party discipline and avoid public criticism. Dissenting Punjab leaders met with AICC general secretary K C Venugopal regarding their grievances. They were instructed to address internal matters through designated organizational channels. Any open dissent was deemed politically detrimental and unacceptable to the party leadership. SC says it will rule on plight of patients who can't afford life-saving drugs and judicial expediency in such cases. SC to take up high cost of lifesaver meds Fees at non-profit medical colleges not regulated, among highest. National Medical Commission regulations have changed regarding medical college establishment. Previously, only Section 8 companies could set up medical colleges. Now, all companies incorporated under the Companies Act can establish medical institutions. This shift follows earlier amendments allowing private companies to enter medical education. Farooq Abdullah murder bid: Jammu court rejects accused's bail plea. A Jammu court rejected Kamal Singh Jamwal's bail plea in the Farooq Abdullah assassination attempt case. The judge cited wider implications for public order and democratic stability in the decision. Jamwal's medical plea was also dismissed, with no evidence of mental disorder presented. Charges were formally framed against Jamwal under attempt to murder and arms act provisions. '7.5 crore students affected in 10 years': Rahul Gandhi calls paper leaks the 'norm' at Chhatron Ki Goonj programme in Dehradun. NEW DELHI: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi on Friday said India's education system has reached a stage where paper leaks have become "the norm", alleging that the issue affects millions of students and requires a political consensus to resolve. What US, Iran, Russia say is irrelevant, we have to march forward with a smile: V-P. The V-P released the book RSS @100: A Century of Service, Unity and Sacrifice by Shyam Jaju and Anupam Trivedi at his official residence here. Demolition order against Azam Khan's varsity vindictive, unjust: AIMPLB. The university is run by the Mohammad Ali Jauhar Trust, which is chaired by jailed former Uttar Pradesh minister and senior SP leader Azam Khan. Ram temple 'donation theft' probe widens: After 8 held, 30 more under lens. Probe finds ad hoc hiring: 'Sensitive work' entrusted through recommendations The NEET topper from J and K: Hadiya Nisar steered clear of negative thoughts – and social media. Hadiya secured rank 99 with a 99.9931 percentile. Eye on delimitation Bill, Government puts draft law seeking removal of 'tainted' ministers on backburner. House panel defers adoption of report on 130th Constitution Amendment Bill, ready for wider consultation PM Modi inaugurates 75 redeveloped railway stations under Amrit Bharat Station Scheme – Full list. PM Narendra Modi has inaugurated 75 redeveloped railway stations under the Amrit Bharat Station Scheme. Amid TMC turmoil, Mamata Banerjee gets a vote of confidence , from Assam. Assam's lone TMC MLA Sherman Ali Ahmed holds meeting with state party workers, leaders and resolves to support embattled party president In launching app for cadre, YSRCP chief Jagan Mohan Reddy follows Vijay's playbook. YSRCP leaders said the "Jagan 2.0 Super App" is a dedicated digital platform that will connect every party worker directly with the leadership and enable them to express their views without fear. Just chill: Bihar unveils 2 AC lounges in Patna for gig workers. The facilities, established by Patna Smart City Ltd, are located at Gandhi Maidan Gate No. Robert De Niro figures in Tarun Tejpal's arguments in court. Former Tehelka editor claims complainant in 2013 sexual assault case has a 'propensity to lie'

transcript.title: Around the world
transcript.text: And finally, here is what is happening around the world. Magnitude 7.3 earthquake quake strikes off Mexico coast. The US Tsunami Warning Center has recorded waves 0.3m (1.1 feet) high in Puerto Madero and Chiapas in Mexico after the quake. Air conditioner sparked deadly blaze at children's orphanage - police. Eleven people, including children, died in the fire that also injured 19 others. Second tanker in three months hijacked off Yemen by suspected Somali pirates. There have also been several unsuccessful attacks in a region where piracy appears to be making a comeback. Trump alleges China meddled in 2020 election and questions voting security ahead of midterms. China said Trump's claims were fabricated and US Democrats said he was paving the way to undermine November's elections.

transcript.title: Sign-off
transcript.text: That is your briefing. Stay sharp, and have a strong day.
